  "findings": [
    "Shield grounded at both ends, creating a ground loop."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Isolate and lift the shield ground at one end (typically at the field instrument) in accordance with the loop drawings, leaving it grounded only at the control panel/DCS end.",
    "Insulate the lifted shield tail with heat-shrink or electrical tape to prevent accidental grounding."
  ],
  "rationale": "Instrument signal cable shields must only be grounded at a single point (typically the control room or marshalling panel) to prevent ground loops, which introduce electrical noise and can corrupt analog signals. Grounding both ends violates standard instrumentation installation specifications and loop drawings.",
  "references": [
    "Loop drawings",
    "ISA 5.1",
    "IEEE 518"
  ]
}
